name: Bambu Lab A1 Mini
description: Best plug-and-play 3D printer for absolute beginners
summary: If you want to unbox a 3D printer and start printing great stuff in under 15 minutes with zero tinkering, the A1 Mini is the one.
pros: Prints perfectly out of the box

name: Creality Ender-3 V3
description: The classic beginner printer, now with auto-leveling
summary: The Ender-3 is THE printer that got millions into 3D printing. The V3 finally adds auto-leveling and a better hotend while keeping the price low.

name: Anycubic Kobra 3
description: Budget multicolor printing with ACE system
summary: If you want multicolor prints without paying Bambu prices, the Kobra 3 with ACE is the best budget option out there.

name: Prusa MK4S
description: The reliability king for serious hobbyists
summary: If you want a printer that just works, every single time, for years, and you do not mind paying more for it, the Prusa MK4S is the gold standard.

name: Elegoo Neptune 4 Pro
description: Fast Klipper-based printer at a great price
summary: If you want speed and do not mind a bit of setup, the Neptune 4 Pro gives you Klipper firmware performance at half the price of premium printers.
cons: Needs firmware updates out of box

name: FlashForge Adventurer 5M Pro
description: Enclosed printer for hassle-free ABS and PETG
summary: If you need to print ABS, nylon, or other tricky materials without building an enclosure yourself, the Adventurer 5M Pro comes ready to go.
